Priority: normal  Level: level1 | [2026-04-29T19:28:12Z] Recommendation: To look for a clinical site location, use Explore Clinical Locations.   From the dashboard, click Explore Clinical Locations. All the locations will be displayed on the map. If you are looking for a specific location, use the search bar. As you type, the list will filter accordingly, and the map will only display pins for Locations on the list. You can also apply filters based on State, City, Zip Code, or Site Name, and you can also filter by Location Area and specific settings. If needed, click Reset All to clear filters. Once you find a Location, click the Location Name to open the drawer and view detailed information about the site.   If a clinical site location does not appear, the help center documentation only states that locations shown in Explore Clinical Locations are locations your program has active contracts with, and that inactive locations will not be shown to students on the Explore Clinical Location page. It also states that administrators can manage locations under Sites by selecting the Site, clicking Locations, and then +Add Location to add a Location’s details. Please note that location name and full address are required before you can save.   The following information is not available in the help center documentation: how to make a specific location appear in Explore & apply for availability, how to make Medstar Georgetown University Hospital available for a DNP student’s clinical rotation, and any documented process specific to Explore & apply for availability. | [2026-04-29T19:28:13Z] ✅ Security Check: Single external domain verified (onlinenursing.baylor.edu). If you get “Invalid Username or Password,” verify that you entered the correct username/email and password, use Forgot Password if needed, and if you are an Exxat Prism user who has not been invited, contact your school/university.   If your account is deactivated due to inactivity, click Activate your account and follow the on-screen instructions.   If your account is inactive, it has been deactivated by your school administrator, and you should contact your school’s IT Support/Services team. Once there, enter your username and continue based on your school type. For non-Prism school students, enter your username, click Continue, go to the password page, and log in to Exxat One. For Prism schools with Exxat credentials, enter your username, click Continue, sign in, and then click Continue on the Exxat One tile. For Prism schools with University SSO credentials, enter your university username, click Continue, complete the university SSO login, and then click Continue on the Exxat One tile. For V3 users, enter your username, click Next, sign in, and then click Continue on the Exxat One tile.   If this is your first time logging in, click Forgot Password, enter your username, click Submit, and follow the instructions in the email to create your password. Once your password is set, return to the login page and sign in using your email and new password. Private Comments: [2026-04-28T15:48:14Z] Recommendation: Before you can begin using Exxat, you’ll need to get added to the system. You will receive an email invitation from Exxat with a link to activate your account. Click the hyperlink in the email. If the link does not work, copy and paste the URL into a new tab or window. Then enter your new password following the guidelines and click Submit. You’ll get a confirmation message letting you know your password setup was successful. After that, you can log in using the email address that received the invitation and your new password.   If your account creation is not going through, the help center documentation says a few specific things to check. If you haven’t activated your account and try to log in, the login screen will prompt you to check your inbox for the invitation email, including the email address it was sent to and the date it was sent. Search your inbox for that invitation email. If you still can’t find it, click Resend Link to receive a new invitation email with the activation link.   If you are getting “Invalid Username or Password,” that means either your username/email or password is incorrect. Verify that you have entered the correct username/email and password, and use Forgot Password if needed. In most cases, your username and email is your school or university email address. If you forgot your username or email, contact your School Administrator or IT Services team to confirm your registered email address. If you are an Exxat Prism user and have not been invited to Exxat Prism, contact your school or university.   If your school uses Single Sign On (SSO), you will use your school credentials to sign onto Exxat. You must activate your account using the activation link sent by email. Then enter your university email address and continue to your school’s login page. If you cannot log in using SSO, first contact your institution’s IT support or help desk. If you are getting the error “Incorrect SSO Key provided by your school,” please contact your school or university’s IT Support, IT Help Desk, or Information Technology Services team to correct the SSO Key shared with Exxat.   If your account is deactivated due to inactivity, click Activate your account and follow the on-screen instructions to regain access. If your account is inactive, that means it has been deactivated by your school administrator, and you should contact your school’s IT Support or Services team to reactivate it. | [2026-04-28T15:48:14Z] The user needs help creating their Exxat account because the account creation process is not going through. On the Compliance window, review the requirement list and statuses such as Get started, In progress, Pending Review, Approved, Not Approved, Expiring, and Expired.   Click anywhere on the row of the item you want to manage, or click the pencil icon under the Action column.   Review the guidelines, template or sample document (if provided), and any previously uploaded information.   Click the plus (+) icon next to the document name to begin uploading a document, or click the pencil icon to edit an existing item.   Enter the required details, which may include:    Start Date   Expiration Date   Note   Upload files      Click Submit for Review.   Click OK to confirm.   The item status will change to Pending Review.     The documentation also says that the documents you need to upload are the compliance items your school has required in your profile. These can include health and immunizations, insurance, certifications, and more. If a specific requirement such as HIPAA, CPR, TB, or release of information has been activated by your school, it will be visible in the Compliance section.   If a document is already in Approved status, you will not be able to re-upload the same document. In that case, click the Approved status or the pencil icon for that requirement and click Add New Record. Priority: normal  Level: level1 | [2026-04-28T13:54:57Z] Recommendation: From the available Exxat documentation, the only explicit guidance I found about why a school would click “Confirm” in relation to Exxat One and Prism is this:     In the Exxat One FAQ for school administrators, Exxat states: “If you publish the placements in Prism, do you still need to confirm? A: Yes, this integration is not live today. You will need to confirm the student in Exxat One for them to get access and be able to being their onboarding.”   The same FAQ also states: “Will ALL students see the Exxat One tile when they login? A. No, the students will not see Exxat One tile unless they have been confirmed by the school.”     Based on that documentation, the documented explanation you can share with the school is that publishing placements in Prism is not enough. The student must be confirmed in Exxat One so they can get access and begin their onboarding, and students will not see the Exxat One tile unless they have been confirmed by the school.   For school-facing login/access documentation, the available help-center article is School Login Guide, which says that school users can go to the Exxat login page, enter their username, and if they have access, they will see the Exxat Prism and Exxat One tiles. To access Exxat One, the article says to click “Continue as School” in the Exxat One tile, which redirects the user to the Exxat One school dashboard.   The following information is not available in the help center documentation:   - the exact step-by-step instructions for where a school program clicks “Confirm” inside the placement workflow   - a help-center article specifically showing the Confirm button on a placement   - documentation stating that clicking Confirm triggers students to enter an Exxat Compliance portal in Prism   - documentation that maps the full workflow between Prism placement confirmation, Exxat One confirmation, and student compliance tasks step by step   What is documented is only that:  - schools using Prism still need to confirm the student in Exxat One  - that confirmation is required for the student to get access and begin their onboarding  - students will not see the Exxat One tile unless they have been confirmed by the school   If you want, I can also turn this into a short email-ready reply you can send directly to the school user. | [2026-04-28T13:54:58Z] ⚠️ Security Alert: Detected 2 different external organizations: concentra.com, shp.rutgers.edu.   🛑 Agent Action: Verify all recipients before replying. Priority: normal  Level: level1 | [2026-04-28T11:32:21Z] Recommendation: Based on the documented Exxat One and Prism help content, the ticket follow-up can be summarized this way:   Location-level access in Exxat One is managed through Personnel and Associated Locations. On the Personnel page, users can be added for tracking, and location associations can then be updated from the personnel record. The documented process is to go to Personnel under site configuration, select the person’s name, and in Associated Locations use the edit option to select or unselect the relevant Locations, Departments, and Units and designate the person as a location coordinator. The documentation also states that personnel can be associated with locations from the Location Profile through Personnel Affiliation, where they can be assigned as Coordinator or Preceptor. This is the documented method for mapping location-level access to the correct clinic locations.   If a requested clinic or location is not already in the system, the documented process is to add it from Locations. In Exxat One for Sites, users go to Site Configuration and then Locations, click + Add Location, and complete the required tabs: Details, Disciplines, and Personnel. In Prism, the documented process is Sites > select the site > Locations > + Add Location. If a student-requested location is not found in Prism, the documented process in Process My Requests is to use Search Location, then Add New Location, and if needed Add New Site.   For confirming whether an existing location with a different name is the same clinic, the following information is not available in the help center documentation: how support should validate that two differently named locations are the same and then merge or confirm them for Exxat One access purposes.   For updating a user’s name when the email already exists under a different first name, the documentation shows that personnel basic details can be edited by opening the person’s record and using the edit pencil in Basic Details. The following information is not available in the help center documentation: how support should resolve a duplicate or existing-email name mismatch scenario. It also includes a Watch Now option. In addition, the FAQ documentation states that Exxat training and webinar schedules are available in the Help section, the “?” icon within Exxat Prism, or on the Exxat website’s Events page.
